For the Fish Marinade: 700 g firm fish fillets (sea bass, kingfish, halibut, or cod)
½ tbsp Kashmiri chili powder (for color & mild heat)
½ tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves, crushed)
For the Spice Paste: 2–3 dried red chilies (adjust to heat preference)
For the Curry Base: 1 tsp mixed seeds (fenugreek + cumin + nigella + black mustard + fennel)

instruction.title: Step 1: Marinate the Fish
steps: Cut fish into medium-sized pieces.
In a bowl, mix turmeric, Kashmiri chili powder, salt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, oil, and crushed kasuri methi.
Coat the fish evenly and marinate for 20–30 minutes.

instruction.title: Step 2: Fry the Fish
steps: Heat 3 tbsp oil in a non-stick pan.
Shallow fry the marinated fish until golden brown on both sides.
Remove fish and set aside. Keep the frying oil for the curry base.

instruction.title: Step 3: Prepare the Spice Paste
steps: Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, dried red chilies, bay leaf, and poppy seeds for 1–2 minutes until aromatic.
Grind roasted spices with ginger, garlic, and ½ cup water into a smooth paste.

instruction.title: Step 4: Cook the Curry Base
steps: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan.
Add the mixed seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the spice paste and sauté 3–4 minutes until raw aroma disappears.
Stir in tomato puree, turmeric, and Kashmiri chili powder. Fry for 2–3 minutes on low-medium flame.
Add salt and cook for 3–4 minutes until oil separates from the masala.

instruction.title: Step 5: Add Water & Simmer
steps: Add 1½ cups water and slit green chilies.
Bring to a gentle boil on high flame, then reduce heat and simmer 5–7 minutes.

instruction.title: Step 6: Add Fried Fish
steps: Carefully add the fried fish pieces into the curry.
Add 1 tbsp reserved frying oil.
Simmer gently on low flame for 6–8 minutes. Avoid stirring too much to keep the fish intact.

instruction.title: Step 7: Final Touch
steps: Check seasoning.
Garnish with a pinch of crushed kasuri methi if desired.
Serve hot with steamed rice or roti.
